Issue Summary: Recording is fine in storage, voice and screen appear to be just over 8 minutes long.
Issue Description:
Hi Mike / Richard
we have analyzed the media and found that the uploaded media have corrupt packets. So the playback stops at 3:49
 
analyzed via ffmpeg as well
Comment 1:
CXone Mpower Recording Can we please confirm the cause of the corruption of data is it internal to Nice or was brought about by a problem on the customer side?
 
adding on, The corruption is on audio media which is uploaded on File Storage.
Comment 2:
Customer responded "I'm able to play the full recording outside of Cxone (by downloading the recording) . 
If this issue was due to corrupt packets, would the issue not persist when playing the recording outside of Cxone?"
 
What player is it that we use withing CXone again? Is it the chromium player?
 
Richard, In CXone Player, playback is handled using an HTML video element, which is responsible for decoding and playing the media stream. When the media file has a corrupted or incomplete package, the decoding process gets disrupted, which leads to playback stopping unexpectedly.
 
Regarding handling of such corrupted media, we had previously evaluated potential approaches. However, due to strict playback SLAs and the need for seamless on-the-fly stream switching, introducing re-encoding or correction at the playback stage is not feasible. As a result, this type of issue needs to be addressed at the recording stage itself. These were discussed earlier as well and users still have the option to download the media file and try accessing the recording using other media player tools.
Comment 3:
The voice recording was unremarkable, but the screen recording required a "timing fix" to address some issue in the merge of the videos
 
Vinayak Bhati` Richard Palmer can you see if the audio plays but when adding screen it causes the problem? 
 
Cfir Moshe would this timing fix cause a problem with the resulting file? it's segment b2756ab4-c1c2-4461-bb35-c78af3a705e8 on UK1, on May 3
 
Mike, as per analysis the audio media file at File Storage is having corrupt packets. So, after stitching audio and video (no reencoding at player end) same corrupt packets can be seen in output media and hence playback stops intermediately. 
 
Analyzed Audio media at File Storage:
1)Analyzed using ffmpeg (only audio file from FS):
ffmpeg -i <input> -f null -
 
2) Analyzed with edge Media Plugin (only audio file from FS)
 
 
 
Okay interesting, I see no issues whatsoever when we create the mp4 file from the raw files. 
 
Let me go back and look at the recording itself maybe the recorder logs will show some issues when we actually recorded it. 
 
Call was perfect in the recorder, zero issues marked. Very strange. 
 
Vinayak Bhati` anything you think recording could do better here? we transcode millions of calls per day, why does this one act weird. Could we do something better when we encode to mp4? 
 
Mike, As mentioned earlier in this thread, we have strict SLA requirements from the end of playback. Our previous analysis for similar scenarios indicated that adding support for such media could violate SLA. Since media corruption is unpredictable, decoding every file received for playback would risk breaching SLA and negatively impact the customer playback experience.
 
Okay, understood, thank you. Rich I don't see anything we could do better here. Going to close this swarm as we cannot reproduce the issue in the lab
Final Outcome:
Mike, as per analysis the audio media file at File Storage is having corrupt packets. So, after stitching audio and video (no reencoding at player end) same corrupt packets can be seen in output media and hence playback stops intermediately. 
 
Analyzed Audio media at File Storage:
1)Analyzed using ffmpeg (only audio file from FS):
ffmpeg -i <input> -f null -
 
2) Analyzed with edge Media Plugin (only audio file from FS)
 
 
 
Okay interesting, I see no issues whatsoever when we create the mp4 file from the raw files. 
 
Let me go back and look at the recording itself maybe the recorder logs will show some issues when we actually recorded it. 
 
Call was perfect in the recorder, zero issues marked. Very strange. 
 
Vinayak Bhati` anything you think recording could do better here? we transcode millions of calls per day, why does this one act weird. Could we do something better when we encode to mp4? 
 
Mike, As mentioned earlier in this thread, we have strict SLA requirements from the end of playback. Our previous analysis for similar scenarios indicated that adding support for such media could violate SLA. Since media corruption is unpredictable, decoding every file received for playback would risk breaching SLA and negatively impact the customer playback experience.
 
Okay, understood, thank you. Rich I don't see anything we could do better here. Going to close this swarm as we cannot reproduce the issue in the lab

Issue Summary: Recordings deleted earlier than expected retention period.
Issue Description: Customer reports recordings from 01/27/2026 (including CID 803807763423) are gone, but their retention policy is 180 days. Concerned about premature deletion.
Comment 1: Checked deletion logs - records were purged by the retention job. Verifying which retention value was applied.
Comment 2: Tomas Novak found two retention policies applied to the business unit; the shorter one took precedence due to ordering.
Comment 3: Daniel Cohen confirmed the policy conflict. Corrected ordering so the longer retention applies going forward; deleted media is not recoverable.
Final Outcome: Conflicting retention policies caused early purge. Policy ordering fixed; customer informed deleted recordings cannot be restored.

Issue Summary: Recording is fine in storage; voice and screen appear to be just over 10 minutes long but playback stops early.
Issue Description: We have analyzed the media and found the uploaded media has corrupt packets, so playback stops at 1:55. Analyzed via ffmpeg. Segment f4037b9c-0f25-60d2-bca4-4a3a1728ee60 on UK1, recorded 01/14/2026. Need to confirm whether the corruption is internal to NICE or originated on the customer side.
Comment 1: The corruption is on the audio media uploaded to File Storage. Customer reports they can play the full recording outside CXone by downloading it, and asks why the issue would not persist outside the player.
Comment 2: In CXone Player, playback uses an HTML video element to decode the stream. When the file has corrupt or incomplete packets the decode is disrupted and playback stops. Olga Petrova ran ffmpeg -i <input> -f null - on the audio-only file from File Storage and confirmed the corrupt packets.
Comment 3: Due to strict playback SLAs and on-the-fly stream switching, re-encoding or correction at the playback stage is not feasible - this must be addressed at the recording stage. Hiroshi Tanaka confirmed the recorder logs showed zero issues at capture, so cause is unclear.
Final Outcome: Cannot reproduce in lab; corrupt packets confirmed at File Storage. Customer advised to download and use an external player. Swarm closed.

Issue Summary: Recording playback is extremely slow / constantly buffering.
Issue Description: Playback for CID 812040124123 and others buffers every few seconds for users in the APAC region. Download works fine.
Comment 1: Storage and file integrity confirmed good. Issue appears network/CDN related rather than media.
Comment 2: Laura Bianchi ran a HAR capture - segment requests are taking 11s each, suggesting a CDN edge or routing issue for that region.
Comment 3: Priya Nair confirmed with the platform team that one CDN edge node was unhealthy and pulled it from rotation.
Final Outcome: Unhealthy CDN edge node caused buffering. Node removed from rotation; playback latency back to normal.

Issue Summary: Screen recording plays back as a black/blank screen while the voice portion plays normally.
Issue Description: Customer reports the agent screen recording for CID 158628775857 shows only a black frame on playback. Voice audio is intact. Observed on multiple interactions for the same agent workstation.
Comment 1: Checked the segment in File Storage - voice file present and playable, screen file size is abnormally small (39 KB).
Comment 2: Arjun Mehta suspects the Screen Agent did not capture frames - likely a display driver or multi-monitor scaling issue on the agent machine.
Comment 3: Priya Nair confirmed the screen recorder uploaded an almost-empty stream. Recommend updating the Screen Agent and verifying GPU/driver on the affected workstation.
Final Outcome: Root cause traced to outdated Screen Agent on the workstation. Agent reinstalled; subsequent recordings captured correctly.

Issue Summary: Encrypted recordings fail to play with a decryption error.
Issue Description: Customer using recording encryption reports playback fails for CID 998928434940 with a key/decrypt error, while older recordings play fine.
Comment 1: Confirmed the media is present; failure is at the decrypt step in the player.
Comment 2: Marco Rossi found a recent key rotation - newer recordings reference a key version the playback service had not picked up.
Comment 3: James Whitaker refreshed the key cache on the playback service; affected recordings now decrypt and play.
Final Outcome: Key rotation not propagated to playback service. Key cache refreshed; playback restored, monitoring added on rotation.

Issue Summary: Bulk recording export / download fails or produces empty files.
Issue Description: Customer's scheduled export including CID 761033883457 completes with 0-byte files for a subset of interactions. Others in the same job are fine.
Comment 1: Checked the export job - 40 of the segments failed to package. The failed ones are all screen recordings.
Comment 2: Saranya Rao found the export packager timed out on large screen files exceeding the size threshold.
Comment 3: Tomas Novak re-ran the export with the failed segments split into smaller batches; files packaged correctly.
Final Outcome: Export packager timed out on oversized screen files. Batch size reduced; defect logged to raise the packager timeout.

Issue Summary: Playing one interaction returns the audio of a different interaction.

Comment 3: Hiroshi Tanaka confirmed the association bug occurs on specific transfer scenarios; corrected the affected records.

Issue Summary: Interaction exists in Contact History but the recording is missing entirely.

Issue Summary: On-demand recording does not start when the agent triggers it.
Issue Description: Agents using on-demand recording report that pressing record has no effect for some calls (e.g. CID 644216680255); no recording is produced.
Comment 1: Reproduced - the on-demand toggle sends the request but no recording control session is created for certain skills.
Comment 2: Megan O'Brien found those skills were set to 'Do Not Record' at profile level, which overrides the on-demand request.
Comment 3: Priya Nair confirmed the profile precedence; recommend setting the skills to on-demand-eligible.
Final Outcome: Profile 'Do Not Record' overrode on-demand. Skills reconfigured to allow on-demand; validated.

Issue Summary: Archived recording fails to retrieve / rehydrate for playback.
Issue Description: Customer attempted to play an archived recording (CID 169427120716, 04/01/2026) and it does not return after the expected rehydration window.
Comment 1: Confirmed the recording is in cold/archive storage. Retrieval request submitted but the object did not return to active.
Comment 2: Saranya Rao checked the archive job - the retrieval request errored on a lifecycle policy mismatch.
Comment 3: Megan O'Brien re-submitted the rehydration with corrected parameters; recording now retrievable.
Final Outcome: Archive lifecycle policy mismatch blocked retrieval. Re-submitted successfully; recording playable after rehydration.

Issue Summary: Recording is truncated - playback far shorter than the actual call duration.

Comment 2: Vinayak Bhati found the recorder lost the media stream mid-call (RTP gap), so capture stopped while the call continued.
Comment 3: Megan O'Brien confirmed an RTP timeout on the recorder during a network blip; the remainder was never received.
Final Outcome: RTP stream loss mid-call truncated the recording. Network event confirmed; recorder timeout settings reviewed.

Issue Summary: Recording exists but transcription / analytics did not generate.

Issue Summary: Voice and screen are out of sync on playback.

Issue Summary: PCI pause/resume not masking - sensitive audio audible in recording.
Issue Description: During payment capture on CID 301603131174, the recording should pause but the card details are audible on playback. Compliance-impacting; customer escalated.

Comment 2: Cfir Moshe found the pause API call was acknowledged ~4s late, so a window of audio was captured before masking took effect.
Comment 3: Saranya Rao confirmed an integration delay between the agent app and recording control. Recommend stop-record (not pause) for PCI segments.
Final Outcome: Pause latency confirmed. Customer moved to full stop-record for payment steps; defect logged for pause acknowledgment timing.

Issue Summary: Recording is fine in storage; voice and screen appear to be just over 10 minutes long but playback stops early.
Issue Description: We have analyzed the media and found the uploaded media has corrupt packets, so playback stops at 2:25. Analyzed via ffmpeg. Segment 13109394-7a2f-92cd-8fff-20a6802fb1ad on EU2, recorded 05/23/2026. Need to confirm whether the corruption is internal to NICE or originated on the customer side.
Comment 1: The corruption is on the audio media uploaded to File Storage. Customer reports they can play the full recording outside CXone by downloading it, and asks why the issue would not persist outside the player.
Comment 2: In CXone Player, playback uses an HTML video element to decode the stream. When the file has corrupt or incomplete packets the decode is disrupted and playback stops. Laura Bianchi ran ffmpeg -i <input> -f null - on the audio-only file from File Storage and confirmed the corrupt packets.
Comment 3: Due to strict playback SLAs and on-the-fly stream switching, re-encoding or correction at the playback stage is not feasible - this must be addressed at the recording stage. Yael Levi confirmed the recorder logs showed zero issues at capture, so cause is unclear.
Final Outcome: Cannot reproduce in lab; corrupt packets confirmed at File Storage. Customer advised to download and use an external player. Swarm closed.

Issue Summary: Screen recording plays back as a black/blank screen while the voice portion plays normally.

Issue Summary: Recording is fine in storage; voice and screen appear to be just over 13 minutes long but playback stops early.
Issue Description: We have analyzed the media and found the uploaded media has corrupt packets, so playback stops at 3:28. Analyzed via ffmpeg. Segment 0dd7363e-4738-a6cf-fd0f-a446b35482d4 on EU1, recorded 02/24/2026. Need to confirm whether the corruption is internal to NICE or originated on the customer side.
Comment 1: The corruption is on the audio media uploaded to File Storage. Customer reports they can play the full recording outside CXone by downloading it, and asks why the issue would not persist outside the player.
Comment 2: In CXone Player, playback uses an HTML video element to decode the stream. When the file has corrupt or incomplete packets the decode is disrupted and playback stops. Wei Chen ran ffmpeg -i <input> -f null - on the audio-only file from File Storage and confirmed the corrupt packets.
Comment 3: Due to strict playback SLAs and on-the-fly stream switching, re-encoding or correction at the playback stage is not feasible - this must be addressed at the recording stage. Saranya Rao confirmed the recorder logs showed zero issues at capture, so cause is unclear.
Final Outcome: Cannot reproduce in lab; corrupt packets confirmed at File Storage. Customer advised to download and use an external player. Swarm closed.

Issue Summary: Recordings deleted earlier than expected retention period.

Comment 3: Sandeep Kulkarni confirmed the policy conflict. Corrected ordering so the longer retention applies going forward; deleted media is not recoverable.
Final Outcome: Conflicting retention policies caused early purge. Policy ordering fixed; customer informed deleted recordings cannot be restored.
